title: Alert missing runbook link — on-call wastes time diagnosing
code_snippet:
- alert: KafkaConsumerLagHigh
  expr: kafka_consumer_group_lag > 10000
  for: 10m
  labels:
    severity: warning
  annotations:
    summary: 'Kafka consumer lag > 10K on {{ $labels.consumer_group }}'
expected_feedback: Standard: Alert fires with a summary but no runbook_url annotation. When on-call receives 'Kafka consumer lag > 10K on order-processor,' their first question is 'what do I do?' Without a runbook, they spend 15+ minutes finding documentation, asking teammates, or guessing. Add annotations.runbook_url pointing to a page with: (1) what this alert means, (2) how to diagnose (check consumer health, check for poison messages, check broker health), (3) immediate mitigation steps (scale consumers, restart stuck consumer, check DLQ).
violated_rule: Runbook link included in alert annotations
